steps.title: Use a comma after the opener
steps.content: The opening clause begins with "Though," so it is dependent and cannot stand alone. It introduces the independent clause "the mission was actually the ninety-fourth flight." When a dependent clause comes before an independent clause, a comma separates them.

steps.title: Reject faulty boundaries
steps.content:
• Choice A adds "and," incorrectly joining a dependent clause as if it were independent.
  • Choice B puts a period after a dependent clause, creating a fragment.
  • Choice C gives no punctuation between the dependent and independent clauses.
The remaining choice supplies the needed comma before "the mission." The answer is D.
